Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex [The ShadowRay group, with basic skill levels, conducts a multi-stage cyber attack targeting healthcare organizations. They begin with reconnaissance, identifying potential entry points via open websites and domains. Following this, they develop resources like phishing servers and email accounts. Initial access is achieved through targeted phishing emails. Execution of malicious payloads follows, leading to persistence via web shells. Privilege escalation occurs through exploitation of application logs. Defense evasion is attempted using obfuscated files. Credential access is sought through brute forcing user accounts. Discovery of the network configuration leads to lateral movement across the network. Sensitive data is collected from local systems before being exfiltrated over command and control channels. Finally, impact is made through data destruction, disrupting operations.]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Social Media Platform, Command & Control Server, User Account, File System, Network Traffic, Target System Artifact, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Web Shell, Application Log Content, User Account Authentication, System Network Configuration Discovery, Network Traffic Flow, File Access, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, File Modification

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the healthcare organization's public-facing websites to understand their structure and potential vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker sets up phishing servers and email accounts to facilitate the delivery of malicious content to the target organization.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends crafted emails to employees mimicking legitimate communications to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once initial access is gained, the attacker executes malicious code to establish a foothold within the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain access, the attacker deploys a web shell on the compromised server to survive reboots and logouts.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker escalates privileges by exploiting weaknesses in the application logs to gain higher-level permissions.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: The attacker uses obfuscation techniques to avoid detection by security tools and personnel.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts to gain valid credentials through repeated login attempts, hoping to eventually succeed.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps the network topology to plan lateral movement to critical systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally across the network to reach critical systems and expand the scope of the breach.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker harvests sensitive data from compromised machines to collect valuable information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker transfers stolen data to attacker-controlled infrastructure using established command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker disrupts operations and causes damage by destroying critical data within the compromised systems.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay group employs a sophisticated, multi-stage cyber attack on healthcare organizations, leveraging basic skills to infiltrate networks, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, and ultimately cause significant impact through data destruction. Their approach involves extensive reconnaissance, resource development, and strategic execution to achieve their objectives while minimizing detection.
